# Appstract

## About

Python application construction helper.

## Installation

```sh
python -m pip install appstract
```

functions.name: appstract.logging._test_helpers.local_logger

It will help a test not to interfere other tests.

See comments in ``logging.Logger._src_file``
for explanation why we didn't use __file__

functions.name: appstract.logging.resources.create_utc_time_without_microsecond
documentation:
Create a UTC time tag without microsecond.

# Notes

`scipp` uses UTC time for time labels.
Microsecond is dropped to keep the log file names readable.
The time tag is intended to be use only for logging,
therefore microsecond information is not necessary.

functions.name: appstract.logging.resources.initialize_log_dir
documentation:
Create the directory including parents if needed and returns True.
Raises `FileExistsError` if `dir_path` is an existing file, not a directory.

functions.name: appstract.logging.resources.validate_log_file_prefix
documentation: Log file prefix should not contain any underscores.

functions.name: appstract.logging.resources.create_log_file_name
documentation: Combine prefix, time_tag and suffix into a new file name.

functions.name: appstract.logging.resources.create_log_file_path
documentation:
Create a log file path joining `parent_dir` and `file_name`.

# Notes

It does not check if the file already exists or not,
therefore a file handler using this path might append logs into an existing file.
This case will be rare since the log file name contains time tag down to seconds,
and another file is not expected to be created within 1 second.

functions.name: appstract.logging.resources.check_file_handlers
documentation:
Raises `RuntimeError` if any files attached to file handlers do not exist.

# Notes

This function is intended to be used only during the initial setup of the daemon
or for the integrated testing, which might create and delete a temporary log file.

functions.name: appstract.logging.providers.get_logger
documentation: Retrieves a default logger and add `stream_handler` if `verbose`.

functions.name: appstract.logging.providers.initialize_file_handler
documentation:
Add a file handler to the `logger`.
It creates the directory at `dir_path` if it does not exist.
File name is automatically created.

To adjust file name with different prefix or extension,
see `create_log_file_path`.

modules.name: appstract.logging.formatters
documentation: SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause
Copyright (c) 2024 Scipp contributors (https://github.com/scipp)

classes.name: appstract.logging.formatters.LogColumn
documentation:
Single column of each log.

# Parameters

variable_name:

: Name of the variable in formatter.

min_length:

: Minimum length of the column. Default is None.

title:

: Title of the column in header line. Capitalized `variable_name` if None.

style:

: Formatting style.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from appstract.logging.formatters import LogColumn
>>> time_column = LogColumn(variable_name='asctime',
... min_length=8, title='TIME', style='{')
>>> time_column.formatter
'{asctime:8}'
>>> time_column.title
'TIME'
>>> app_name_column = LogColumn(variable_name='app_name',
... title='APPLICATION', style='{')
>>> app_name_column.formatter
'{app_name}'
>>> app_name_column.title
'APPLICATION'
```

classes.name: appstract.logging.formatters.LogHeader
documentation:
Formatter constructor from a group of {class}`~LogColumn` s.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from appstract.logging.formatters import LogHeader, LogColumn
>>> time_column = LogColumn(variable_name='asctime',
... min_length=8, title='TIME', style='{')
>>> app_name_column = LogColumn(variable_name='app_name',
... title='APPLICATION', style='{')
>>> header = LogHeader(time_column, app_name_column)
>>> header.fmt
'{asctime:8} | {app_name}'
>>> header.format().replace(' ', '_')
'TIME_____|_APPLICATION'
```

Helper functions for parsing type hints and annotation of a callable object.

functions.name: appstract.constructors.inspectors.validate_annotation
documentation:
Check if the origin of the annotation is not Union.

If it for later implementation of Union type handling.

functions.name: appstract.constructors.inspectors.get_product_spec
documentation:
Retrieve the product of provider.
If `callable_obj` is a function, it is a return type annotation,
and if `callable_obj` is a class, it is the class itself.

functions.name: appstract.constructors.inspectors.collect_argument_specs
documentation:
Collect Dependencies from the signature and type hints.
`default_args` and `default_keywords` will overwrite the annotation.

functions.name: appstract.constructors.inspectors.collect_attr_specs
documentation:
Collect Dependencies from the type hints of the class.
It ignores any attributes without type hints.

documentation: Specification of a product (returned value) of a provider.

classes.name: appstract.constructors.inspectors.DependencySpec
documentation: Specification of sub-dependencies (arguments/attributes) of a provider.

functions.name: appstract.constructors.factories.multiple_constant_providers
documentation: Create and yield a new factory containing a copy of all given constants.

functions.name: appstract.constructors.factories.multiple_temporary_providers
documentation: Create and yield a new factory containing a copy of all given providers.

classes.name: appstract.constructors.factories.Factory

methods.name: appstract.constructors.factories.Factory.__init__
documentation: Initializes a factory with multiple provider groups.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.factories.Factory.catalogue
documentation: Frozen set of the product type(s) this factory can manufacture.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.factories.Factory.__iter__
documentation: Return an iterator of the product type(s) this factory can manufacture.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.factories.Factory.__len__
documentation: Return the number of product type(s) this factory can manufacture.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.factories.Factory._call_provider
documentation:
Build keyword argument dependencies of the provider
and call the provider with them.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.factories.Factory._inject_attributes
documentation:
Build and inject attribute dependencies of the `product`.
If `product` is missing any attributes that are type-hinted,
the attribute(dependency) will be assembled and injected.

# Raises

ProviderNotFoundError

: If any type-hinted attributes are not populated in `product` and
  any providers for the missing attributes are not found.

# Notes

When the `product` already has the attribute but it is `None`,
: 1. if a provider is not found, it skips injection.
  2. if a provider is found, it overwrites the attribute.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.factories.Factory.__getitem__
documentation:
Build an object of `product_type` using the registered providers.

# Notes

Currently position-only argument dependencies are not supported.
It only builds the keyword argument dependencies
and attribute dependencies for the provider.

TODO: Detect cyclic dependencies earlier.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.factories.Factory.local_factory
documentation:
Create and yield a new factory containing
a copy of `self.providers` and `provider_groups`.
For existing providers, `provider_groups` will overwrite them.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.factories.Factory.constant_provider
documentation:
Use a lambda function that returns `hardcoded_value`.
as a temporary provider of `product_type`.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.factories.Factory.partial_provider
documentation:
Create a partial provider for `product_type` with `args` and `kwargs`
and use it as a temporary provider.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.factories.Factory.temporary_provider
documentation:
Replace an existing provider of `product_type` with `temp_provider`
or register `temp_provider`.
`temp_provider` will be replaced with the original provider or removed
as the context terminates.

Dependency injection object.

# Notes

Currently it does not inject dependencies for magic variables,
i.e. `*args` and `**kwargs`.

modules.name: appstract.constructors.providers
documentation: SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause
Copyright (c) 2023 Scipp contributors (https://github.com/scipp)

functions.name: appstract.constructors.providers._validate_callable_as_provider
documentation:
# Raises

NotImplementedError

: If the constructor of a provider is a method of a class.

# Notes

It is not supported to use a method of a class as a provider,
because it is not possible to check
if a `callable_obj` is bound to an instantiated object or just a class
without an access to the object containing the method.
Currently, it is checking if `__qualname__` is different from `__name__`
to see if the `callable_obj` is a method.

TODO: Update the if statement with explicit check for a method.

functions.name: appstract.constructors.providers.split_sequence_by_filter
documentation:
Split a sequence into two sequences based on the filter.

The first one of returned sequences contains items that pass the filter.
The other sequence contains the rest.

functions.name: appstract.constructors.providers.split_dict_by_filter
documentation:
Split a dictionary into two dictionaries based on the filter.

The first one of returned dictionaries contains items that pass the filter.
The other dictionary contains the rest.

functions.name: appstract.constructors.providers.check_conflicting_providers
documentation: Raise an error is given factories have any conflicting providers.

functions.name: appstract.constructors.providers.merge
documentation: Return a new `ProviderGroup` containing all providers of `prov_grs`.

classes.name: appstract.constructors.providers.Provider

methods.name: appstract.constructors.providers.Provider.__init__
documentation:
# Notes

Nested `Provider` or `partial` as a `constructor`
is forbidden similar to `partial`.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.providers.Provider.can_provide
documentation:
Check if the given `product_type` can be supported by this provider.
Product type should be the same type as the returned type of this provider.
If they are not the same types,
the requested product type should be the parent class
of returned type of this provider.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.providers.Provider.__call__
documentation: Call the constructor with extra arguments.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.providers.Provider.__eq__
documentation: Compare `constructor`, `args` and `keywords` of the provider.

Function wrapper that provides certain type of product.

It is similar to `partial` but in addition, it contains
argument or attribute dependency information of the wrapped callable object.

classes.name: appstract.constructors.providers._ArgumentsInstanceFilter
documentation: Filter arguments by instance equality.

classes.name: appstract.constructors.providers._ArgumentsHashFilter
documentation: Filter arguments by hash keys.

classes.name: appstract.constructors.providers.ArgumentsFilter

methods.name: appstract.constructors.providers.ArgumentsFilter.__call__
documentation:
Saves the arguments and returns `True` on the first call.
The second time it is called, it checks if the new arguments match the old ones.

It checks instance equalities of all arguments first
and separate hashable arguments from unhashable ones if it fails.
For hashable arguments, it uses `lru_cache` and
for unhashable arguments, it checks instance equalities.

# Returns

`True`

: If it is the first call or the new arguments match the first ones.

`False`

: If the new arguments don't match the first ones.
Remember arguments and check if the new arguments match the old ones.

classes.name: appstract.constructors.providers.SingletonProvider
documentation:
Singleton provider always returns the same instance.

Therefore passing different arguments from the first call is not allowed.
`functools.lru_cache` is used to check if the hashable arguments are the same.
For unhashable arguments, `cached_arguments_filter`
check if they are the same instances.

`SingletonProvider` is needed for sharing the same object by multiple objects.
The arguments filter prioritizes instance equality over hash equality.
If the hashable arguments check is not compatible,
consider providing the dependencies also as singletons.

# Raises

SingletonProviderCalledWithDifferentArgs

: If the provider is called with different argument from the first call.

classes.name: appstract.constructors.providers.ProviderGroup

methods.name: appstract.constructors.providers.ProviderGroup.__init__
documentation:
Initializes an empty internal provider dictionary
and fills it with the initial providers from the argument.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.providers.ProviderGroup.__copy__
documentation: Return a new provider group containing same providers.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.providers.ProviderGroup.__iter__
documentation: Return an iterator of the product type(s) this group can provide.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.providers.ProviderGroup.__len__
documentation: Return the number of product type(s) this group can provide.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.providers.ProviderGroup.merge
documentation: Merge other provider groups into this group after checking conflicts.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.providers.ProviderGroup.__add__
documentation: Return a new group containing all providers of two groups.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.providers.ProviderGroup.pop
documentation:
Remove and return the provider of `product_type`.
Return `UnknownProvider` if not found.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.providers.ProviderGroup.clear
documentation: Clear all providers of this group.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.providers.ProviderGroup._validate_and_register
documentation:
Validate a provider and add the provider if valid.

# Raises

ProviderExistsError

: If there is an existing provider of the `product_type`.

MismatchingProductTypeError

: If the return type of the `provider` is not a subclass of
  ``` product_type``or the same object as ``product_type ```.
  See `Provider.can_provide`.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.providers.ProviderGroup.__getitem__
documentation:
Return the provider of the requested product type.

Raises
------
ProviderNotFoundError
    If there is any providers for the requested product type.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.providers.ProviderGroup._wrap_provider
documentation: Wrap a callable object with a `Provider` or its subclass.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.providers.ProviderGroup.__setitem__
documentation:
Register a callable object `provider_call` as a provider of `product_type`.
See `_validate_and_register` for new provider validation.

# Notes

If the new provider is same as the existing one,
it will register the new callable object as the provider
instead of raising `ProviderExistsError`.

methods.name: appstract.constructors.providers.ProviderGroup.provider

returns.name:
(
    Callable[
        [Callable[..., Product] | type[Product]],
        Callable[..., Product] | type[Product],
    ]
    | Callable[..., Product]
    | type[Product]
)

documentation:
Register the decorated callable into this group.
The product type will be retrieved from the annotation.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from appstract.constructors import ProviderGroup
>>> from typing import Literal
>>> number_providers = ProviderGroup()
>>> @number_providers.provider
... def give_one() -> Literal[1]:
...   return 1
...
>>> number_providers[Literal[1]]() == 1
True
```
Group of providers.
